_comment: Central configuration for the cross-engine inference benchmark. EVERY setting (host paths, model / scenario / engine / backend registries, and run defaults) lives here rather than in code. Edit this file to retarget the harness; override individual selections at run time with run_matrix.py command-line flags (CLI > env > this file > built-in fallback). Pick a different file with --config PATH or the BENCH_CONFIG env var. Path strings may use ${repo_root}, ${here}, ${model_root}, ${gemma4_qat_dir} placeholders. A path may also be written as {"path": "...", "env": "BENCH_X"} so the named environment variable can override it for host retargeting. The `backends` section is a registry of concrete compute backends (ggml_cuda / ggml_vulkan / ggml_metal / ggml_cpu / cpu / ...); pick which to run with --backends or defaults.backends (see the registry's own _comment for the entry format; the legacy list+maps form from older configs is still accepted). The long-prompt PREFILL sweep (prefill_2k / 4k / 8k / 16k / 32k / 64k / 128k) is part of this matrix: each prefill scenario slices assets/prefill_corpus.txt to a target token budget (scenarios._prefill) and is timed by TTFT (max_tokens=8); run_matrix.py auto-raises llama.cpp's context to fit whichever prefill lengths are selected (the long-context 32k/64k/128k lengths are registered but not in the default selection). For a prefill-only run that excludes the multimodal/diffusion scenarios, use --config benchmark_config_prefill.json instead.

text_short.description: Short single-turn text prompt (~2k-token context preamble)

text_long.description: Long-context single-turn text prompt (~2k-token context preamble + passage)

multi_turn.description: 3-turn conversation (~2k-token context preamble), timed on the final turn

json_mode.description: Structured output (response_format json_object) with ~2k-token context preamble

image_edit.description: Stable-diffusion image editing (Qwen-Image-Edit 2511, Lightning 4-step, cfg 1): same input image, prompt, resolution, steps and seed for every engine

edit.prompt: Change the background to a sunny beach.

prefill_2k.description: Prefill throughput @ ~2k prompt tokens (sliced from prefill_corpus.txt)

prefill_4k.description: Prefill throughput @ ~4k prompt tokens (sliced from prefill_corpus.txt)

prefill_8k.description: Prefill throughput @ ~8k prompt tokens (sliced from prefill_corpus.txt)

prefill_16k.description: Prefill throughput @ ~16k prompt tokens (sliced from prefill_corpus.txt)

prefill_32k.description: Prefill throughput @ ~32k prompt tokens (sliced from prefill_corpus.txt)

prefill_64k.description: Prefill throughput @ ~64k prompt tokens (sliced from prefill_corpus.txt)

prefill_128k.description: Prefill throughput @ ~128k prompt tokens (sliced from prefill_corpus.txt)

backends._comment: Backend registry: one entry per concrete compute backend selectable with --backends (each becomes a report column). `kind` (gpu|cpu) drives generic gating (large models skip cpu-kind; engines.*.backends may list ids and/or kinds). Per-engine sub-objects say how that engine launches on the backend — omit the sub-object and the engine's cells are recorded as skipped. tensorsharp: {backend: --backend value, extra_args: [...], env: {...}}; llamacpp: {ngl: -ngl value, server_exe: per-backend llama-server build (path/env form supported; falls back to paths.llama_server_exe), extra_args, env}; vllm: true marks the column the external vLLM endpoint is comparable on. `aliases` lets old names keep working (legacy `gpu`). NOTE: TensorSharp.Server silently falls back to the first available backend when the requested one is not supported by the build/host — check results/logs/*.log if numbers look implausible.
